topic.what: Comparative evaluation of differential privacy, NER, and LLM methods for de-identifying Dutch clinical notes
topic.how: Tested 5 pipelines (PNER, PLLM, PDP, PNER→DP, PLLM→DP) with 3 DP mechanisms (Metric-DP, RANTEXT) and 5 LLMs (GPT-4o, DeepSeek-R1 8B/70B, LLaMA-3.1 8B, MedGEMMA 27B) measuring privacy leakage and downstream utility on entity/relation classification
topic.why_matters: Enables GDPR/HIPAA-compliant secondary use of Dutch healthcare data; addresses gap in non-English clinical de-identification research

characteristics.what_it_measures: Residual PII percentage in de-identified text vs manual reference
characteristics.context: Metric-DP leaks substantially less than RANTEXT at comparable ϵ

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 for drug/disorder entity classification on de-identified notes
characteristics.vs_baseline: LLM baseline ~1.0; DP pipelines recover 70-95% of baseline at high ϵ
characteristics.context: Utility degrades severely at ϵ≤32; recovers near baseline at ϵ≥512

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 for adverse drug event relation classification
characteristics.vs_baseline: LLM baseline ~0.9; DP pipelines severely harmed even at high ϵ
characteristics.context: Relation classification more sensitive to token perturbation than entity classification; cannot fully recover baseline performance

characteristics.what_it_measures: DP privacy parameter controlling noise magnitude
characteristics.vs_baseline: Standard DP ranges; ϵ>10 considered weak theoretical guarantees in practice
characteristics.context: Trade-off: lower ϵ = stronger privacy but worse utility

characteristics.what_it_measures: Leakage of directly identifying entities (names, phone numbers, DOB)
characteristics.context: Direct PII leakage most damaging; LLM preprocessing reduces exposed sensitive content

characteristics.what_itasures: Manual de-identification time for gold-standard corpus
characteristics.context: Highlights operational advantage of automated methods; manual gold-standard still needed for evaluation

not_for: Formal privacy proofs (empirical proxy metric only)
requires: Privacy-compliant API access (for GPT-4o) or open-source LLM deployment
Annotated Dutch clinical corpus for evaluation
High-quality NER or LLM for preprocessing stage

novelty: First comparative study of DP, NER, and LLMs for Dutch clinical text de-identification
First DP-based text anonymization study on real-world Dutch hospital notes
Hybrid pipeline design combining LLM/NER preprocessing with DP
Explicit comparison of Metric-DP vs RANTEXT for clinical text
open_problems: Language expansion beyond Dutch to assess cross-lingual generalizability
Public benchmark availability for external validation (current dataset is private)
Broader utility evaluation beyond entity/relation classification (e.g., document-level tasks)
Adversarial re-identification risk assessment and quasi-identifier targeting
Reducing computational requirements for privacy-critical settings
Mitigating LLM hallucination risks in de-identification output
